hero.title: Almaty in numbers
hero.subtitle: Energy, water and carbon — monitored in one place so the city can act on what matters.

charts.empty: No series available for this period

carbonPulse.title: Live Carbon Counter
carbonPulse.subtitle: Estimated CO₂ emitted by Almaty's power grid since you opened this page
carbonPulse.basedOn: Based on current annual consumption ≈ {{value}} mln kWh
carbonPulse.footnote: ~85% of Kazakhstan's electricity is generated from coal and gas

heatmap.title: Consumption Heatmap
heatmap.subtitle: Monthly intensity — darker cells mean higher load

energy-growth-high.title: High growth in electricity consumption
energy-growth-high.description: Consumption rose by {{delta}}% compared to the previous period ({{prev}} → {{last}} mln kWh).

energy-growth-moderate.title: Moderate growth in electricity consumption
energy-growth-moderate.description: Consumption rose by {{delta}}% ({{prev}} → {{last}} mln kWh). Worth auditing system efficiency.

energy-reduction.title: Electricity consumption is down
energy-reduction.description: Consumption dropped by {{delta}}% ({{prev}} → {{last}} mln kWh). Efficiency measures are paying off.

water-growth-high.title: Significant growth in water consumption
water-growth-high.description: Water supply volume rose by {{delta}}% ({{prev}} → {{last}} mln m³). Likely network losses or load growth.

water-growth-moderate.title: Water consumption is rising
water-growth-moderate.description: Water supply volume rose by {{delta}}% ({{prev}} → {{last}} mln m³).

water-reduction.title: Water consumption is down
water-reduction.description: Water supply volume fell by {{delta}}% ({{prev}} → {{last}} mln m³).

carbon-footprint-info.title: Power-grid carbon footprint
carbon-footprint-info.description: At current consumption of {{last}} mln kWh, estimated CO₂ emissions reach ~{{carbon}} mln kg. Kazakhstan generates ~85% of its electricity from coal and gas.
